chapter_title.ar: بَابُ فَضْلِ الْحَجِّ وَ الْعُمْرَةِ وَ ثَوَابِهِمَا
chapter_title.en: Chapter 28 – Merits of the Hajj and the Umra, and their Rewards

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (17/127)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (17/127)
text: الْحَاجُّ وَ الْمُعْتَمِرُ فِي ضَمَانِ اللَّهِ فَإِنْ مَاتَ مُتَوَجِّهاً غَفَرَ اللَّهُ لَهُ ذُنُوبَهُ وَ إِنْ مَاتَ مُحْرِماً بَعَثَهُ اللَّهُ مُلَبِّياً وَ إِنْ مَاتَ بِأَحَدِ الْحَرَمَيْنِ بَعَثَهُ اللَّهُ مِنَ الْآمِنِينَ وَ إِنْ مَاتَ مُنْصَرِفاً غَفَرَ اللَّهُ لَهُ جَمِيعَ ذُنُوبِهِ .

en.hubeali: Muhammad Bin yahya, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Muhammad Bin Isa, from Zakariyya Al momin, from Shuayb Al Aqarquqy, from Abu Baseer,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> having said: ‘The pilgrim of the Hajj and the pilgrim of the Umra is in the Guarantee of Allah<sup>azwj</sup>. So if he were to die heading for it, Allah<sup>azwj</sup> would Forgive his sins for him; but if he were to die in Ihram, Allah<sup>azwj</sup> would Resurrect him exclaiming Talbiyya; and if he were to die in one of the two Sanctuaries, Allah<sup>azwj</sup> would Resurrect him among the secured ones; and if he were to die on his way back, Allah<sup>azwj</sup> would Forgive for him the entirety of his sins’.
en.sarwar: 18. Muhammad ibn Yahya has narrated from Ahmad ibn Muhammad from Muhammad ibn ‘Isa from Zakariya al-Mu’min from Shu’ayb al-‘Aqarqufiy from abu Basir who has said the following: “Abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said that the pilgrim’s performing al-Hajj and al-‘Umrah is part of the undertaking of Allah. If he dies when on his way, Allah forgives his sins. If he dies in the state of Ihram, Allah raises him saying Talbiyah (here I am O Lord, to obey Your command). If he dies in one of the two sacred places, Allah raises him well protected. If he dies on his way back home, Allah forgives all of his sins.”
